name: Marble Scones- Country Cooking
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Grease a large baking sheet.
In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and baking soda. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut the butter into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs. Remove 1 1/4 cups of this flour mixture to a medium-sized bowl.
2. In a small bowl, mix buttermilk and vanilla. In another small bowl, combine 1/4 cup of the buttermilk mixture with the melted chocolate. Add the remaining buttermilk mixture to the flour mixture in the large bowl and mix lightly with a fork until it forms a soft dough. Add the chocolate mixture to the reserved 1 1/4 cups of flour mixture in the medium-sized bowl and stir with a fork until it forms a soft dough.
3. Turn both doughs out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide the chocolate dough into 6 pieces. Dot 3 chocolate pieces over the surface of the plain dough. Fold the plain dough over and dot with the remaining 3 pieces of chocolate dough; knead 5 or 6 times to marbleize the dough.
4. Divide the dough in half. Using a lightly floured rolling pin, roll one half of the dough into a 7-inch round. Cut into 4 wedges. Repeat with the remaining half of the dough.
5. Place the scones, about 1 inch apart, on the greased baking sheet. Pierce the tops with the tines of a fork.
Bake scones for 15 to 18 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm.
